title: stelis insectifera
intro: Stelis insectifera, also known as the Ant Plant or Insectivorous Plant, is a unique, carnivorous species of flowering plant that obtains essential nutrients by capturing and digesting insects. The plant's leaves have sticky mucus that traps insects, which are then broken down into nutrients, such as nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium. In traditional medicine, Stelis insectifera is used to treat various ailments, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, often in the form of infusions or decoctions, are said to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support overall health and well-being.
